recommendation.title: Full rest — TSB still well past -25 at -31.7
recommendation.reasoning: TSB is -31.7, past the -25 'swap to easy' threshold. This week's load is real and front-loaded: Jul 4's ~8.5h effort (186.9 TSS) plus Jul 6's swim-plus-commute day (85.9 TSS) plus today's run pushed ATL to 42.6 against a CTL of only 10.9. HRV is 61ms, 8% below your 66ms baseline — mild but consistent with unresolved fatigue. Sleep was good (score 92) and resting HR is normal (43bpm). Tomorrow is already a scheduled rest day — the right call, and the numbers say it's earned, not optional.
recommendation.sessionDetail: No training scheduled. Optional light walk or mobility work if you want to move, nothing structured.
recommendation.bikeNote: It's Saturday — no work commute to plan around. Easy bike or a walk is fine if you want to move, but there's no session to build the choice around.

sessions.lesson: Pace was right at the easy target (5:22/km) but avg HR of 153bpm is well above the ~114-142bpm easy zone — a tempo-effort heart rate at an easy pace, consistent with the accumulated fatigue (TSB -31.7) rather than the effort itself being wrong.
Swim class as scheduled, avg HR 112bpm — a normal aerobic pool effort, nothing to flag here.
An 8.5-hour low-intensity effort (avg HR 89bpm) — the cost here was cumulative duration and calories (2251kcal), not intensity, and it's still the single biggest contributor to this week's fatigue.

recentActivity.analysis: This week's TSS has been front-loaded by Jul 4's ~8.5h effort and Jul 6's swim-plus-commute day, with today's run adding real strain at an unexpectedly high HR — total load is well above what a base-phase week should carry, and TSB (-31.7) reflects that directly. Two of the three sessions this window ran hotter or heavier than they first appeared once the data was corrected, not because the training itself changed.
